purpose: Validate Mira Core server intent queue v0 as intent-record preparation and validation only for a future server/local-arm handoff. Phase 8 consumes server-upload envelope refs, Pulse cards/proposals, and user/offline requests, then emits validated intent records and a validation report without creating a real queue, routing, leasing, executing, sending, deploying, trading, or mutating files.

scope: Build a static validator for future Core intent records.
Consume server-upload envelope refs, Pulse cards/proposals, and user/offline request metadata.
Accept only Tier 0 and Tier 1 intents as pending_local_acceptance in v0.
Route future server-originated intents to local Architect for review/acceptance only.
Keep direct cross-device Builder/Oracle targeting blocked.
Block or require review for Tier 2, Tier 3, Tier 4, identity-core rewrites, secrets/auth changes, deploys, customer sends, financial actions, and local execution requests.
Preserve idempotency and dedupe semantics without creating a queue or causing side effects.
Preserve redaction and profile/session/device scope on every intent record.
hardRejects: Any real queue is created or written.

Any network request, route, lease, local execution, file mutation, source-store write, memory commit, profile commit, external send, customer send, deploy, trade, financial action, secret generation, or auth mutation occurs.
Tier 2, Tier 3, Tier 4, identity-core rewrite, secrets/auth, deploy, customer-send, financial, or local-execution request is accepted as pending_local_acceptance.
Any intent targets Builder or Oracle directly from the future server/cross-device boundary.
Any intent claims the server can execute local shell, operate PTY, deploy, send customer messages, trade, or prove model processing.
Any raw request payload, secret, terminal log, browser state, screenshot/OCR text, customer private data, or side-profile content appears in payload_summary or validation notes.
Any stale or expired intent is accepted.
Any idempotency_key or dedupe_key depends on created_at, intent_id, or generated validation ids.
Any retry duplicates a side effect.

payloadSummaryRules: payload_summary must be short, redacted, and non-authoritative.
payload_summary must not contain raw request bodies, secrets, customer private data, terminal logs, screenshot/OCR text, browser state, or side-profile content.
payload_hash is computed from the redacted canonical payload, not raw private content.
payload_redaction_status must be applied or blocked when input payload contained private content.

localAcceptanceFlow: Future server stores or presents an intent record only after this validator marks it valid.
Local Architect reviews and accepts/rejects the intent after reconnect.
Local Architect may delegate to local Builder or Oracle only after local acceptance and current risk recheck.
Builder and Oracle are never direct cross-device server targets in v0.

requires: safe_next_action does not mutate files or send externally

reviewRequiredOrBlockedV0.reason: Repo mutation remains local-only and requires fresh local Architect review.
External side effects require explicit local proof and user-facing gates outside v0.
Financial or irreversible actions are blocked unless a fresh explicit lane is opened with current proof.

canonicalization: Sort source_refs by source_type then source_id.
Use redacted canonical payload for payload_hash.
Include schema and version in idempotency_key input.
Exclude intent_id, created_at, validation_run_id, and generated_at from idempotency and dedupe input.
idempotencyAndDedupeRules.retryRule: Retries with the same idempotency_key and dedupe_key cannot duplicate side effects because v0 performs no queue write, route, lease, execution, send, deploy, trade, memory commit, profile commit, or file mutation.

redactionPolicy.summaryOnlyRule: Intent payloads are summary-plus-hash only; raw offline request bodies remain local/private and are never copied into intent_records.

staticValidationRules.rule: Output must contain intent_records and validation_report with all required fields.
Only Tier 0 and Tier 1 intents may have status=pending_local_acceptance in v0.
Tier 2 must be review_required or blocked; Tier 3/Tier 4 must be blocked.
Server-originated accepted records must target architect only, with direct Builder/Oracle targets blocked.
Intent records cannot claim server local execution, PTY, deploy, customer-send, trade, or model-processing-proof capability.
Every intent record and source_ref must carry profile, sessionId, and deviceId; mismatches are blocked.
Expired intents are expired/rejected, and stale source refs cannot be pending_local_acceptance.
Payload summary and validation notes must exclude raw private content, secrets, terminal logs, browser state, screenshot/OCR, customer private data, and side-profile content.
Same source/profile/device/risk/action/payload produces stable idempotency_key and dedupe_key; changed payload or scope changes them.
Blocked or review-required records must include a safe_next_action that does not itself mutate, execute, route, send, deploy, trade, or write to a queue.

Validation report must prove no queue, enqueue, network, route, lease, execution, external-send, source-store write, memory/profile commit, file mutation, deploy, customer-send, or trade side effects.
Tests can validate fixture completeness, required cases, required fields, forbidden substrings, counts, and deterministic idempotency/dedupe rules without calling a model.

acceptanceChecks.focus: Tier 0 read-only research intent accepted for local Architect review only

expectedRecord.safe_next_action: Local Architect may accept the read-only research intent after reconnect.

acceptanceChecks.focus: Tier 0 offline reminder accepted as pending local acceptance with expiry

request.payload_summary: Remind local Architect to review Mira Core acceptance fixtures.

acceptanceChecks.focus: Tier 3/Tier 4 side effects blocked with safe alternatives

expectedRecords.blocked_because: Customer-facing sends are external side effects and cannot enter the v0 intent queue as pending local acceptance.
expectedRecords.safe_next_action: Prepare a non-sent draft or read-only customer context note for local review.

expectedRecords.blocked_because: Deploys are Tier 3 external side effects and are outside Phase 8 v0.
expectedRecords.safe_next_action: Prepare a deploy readiness checklist without deploying.

expectedRecords.blocked_because: Financial or irreversible actions are blocked unless a fresh explicit lane is opened with current proof.

acceptanceChecks.focus: Tier 2 repo mutation cannot be pending local acceptance in v0

request.payload_summary: Edit files for Mira Core implementation.

expectedRecord.safe_next_action: Prepare a read-only implementation plan or acceptance checklist for local Architect review.

acceptanceChecks.focus: identity rewrites, secrets/auth, and local execution blocked

expectedRecords.blocked_because: Identity-core rewrites cannot be queued or committed in Phase 8.
expectedRecords.safe_next_action: Create a review-only identity proposal with evidence and eval refs.

expectedRecords.blocked_because: Secrets/auth changes are blocked in Phase 8.
expectedRecords.safe_next_action: Prepare a redacted checklist of required auth facts for local review.

expectedRecords.blocked_because: Future server cannot execute local shell or PTY actions.
expectedRecords.safe_next_action: Ask local Architect to review the request after reconnect.

expectedRecords.blocked_because: Server-originated direct Builder targeting is blocked in v0.
expectedRecords.safe_next_action: Target local Architect for review; local Architect may delegate after reconnect.

expectedRecords.blocked_because: Server-originated direct Oracle targeting is blocked in v0.
expectedRecords.safe_next_action: Target local Architect for review; local Architect may delegate after reconnect.

expectedRecord.blocked_because: Profile/session/device scope does not match the source envelope scope.
expectedRecord.safe_next_action: Keep the request local to its source profile or create a separately scoped intent.

acceptanceChecks.focus: stale source refs and expired intents do not become pending

expectedRecord.safe_next_action: Drop or ask local Architect to review a fresh request after reconnect.

acceptanceChecks.focus: payload summary remains redacted and raw body is blocked

expectedRecord.payload_summary: Redacted request summary unavailable because payload contains blocked private content.

acceptanceChecks.focus: same request dedupes, changed payload or scope changes keys
intent record prep does not queue, route, lease, execute, send, or mutate

summaryAcceptanceCriteria: Phase 8 is intent-record preparation and validation only for a future Core server/local-arm handoff.

No runtime implementation, server, network, queue creation, enqueue, routing, lease, source-store write, memory/profile commit, local execution, external send, deploy, trade, financial action, or file mutation occurs.
The output contains intent_records and validation_report.

Intent records include intent_id, idempotency_key, created_at, profile, sessionId, deviceId, source_refs, requested_by, target_role, allowed_target_roles, risk_tier, action_class, status, local_arm_required, review_required, expires_at, dedupe_key, payload_summary, payload_hash, payload_redaction_status, evidenceRefs, safe_next_action, blocked_because when blocked, and no_execution_performed=true.
Only Tier 0 and Tier 1 intents may be accepted as pending_local_acceptance in v0.
Tier 2 repo mutation is review_required or blocked, never pending_local_acceptance.
Tier 3 external side effects and Tier 4 financial/irreversible actions are blocked with safe alternatives.
Identity-core rewrites, secrets/auth changes, deploys, customer sends, local shell/PTY execution, and financial actions are blocked.
Future server-originated accepted intents target local Architect only; direct Builder/Oracle targeting remains blocked.
Local Architect may delegate to local Builder/Oracle only after reconnect, local acceptance, and current risk recheck.
Server capability truth remains explicit: serverCanExecuteLocal=false and no server PTY, shell, deploy, customer-send, trade, or model-processing-proof claims.
Same source/profile/device/risk/action/payload produces stable idempotency_key and dedupe_key; changed payload or scope changes them.
Retries cannot duplicate side effects because v0 performs no side effects.
Profile/session/device mismatch, stale source refs, and expired intents fail closed.
Payload summaries are redacted summary-plus-hash only; raw private payload content is blocked.
Tests can validate fixture completeness without model calls.
